_meta.project: — Text Recognition and Intelligent Speech Helper Assistant
_meta.usage: Run import_github.sh or use import_github.py to create all issues via the GitHub API.

labels.name: p1-must-have
labels.description: MoSCoW P1 — required for panel defense

labels.name: ocr
labels.description: OCR — ML Kit or Vision API

milestones.title: Phase 1 — Setup & Planning
milestones.description: Weeks 1–3: All three projects scaffolded, Atlas connected, Chapter 1 drafted.

milestones.title: Phase 2 — Core Mobile Features
milestones.description: Weeks 4–7: Flutter OCR, TTS, voice commands, gesture nav, offline mode complete.

milestones.title: Phase 3 — Backend API
milestones.description: Weeks 8–11: All Express modules, MongoDB schemas, deployed to Render.com.

milestones.title: Phase 4 — React Web Portal
milestones.description: Weeks 12–15: Public landing site and superadmin panel deployed to Vercel.

milestones.title: Phase 5 — AI Integration & System Sync
milestones.description: Weeks 16–18: AI chat live, full E2E tested, performance audited.

milestones.title: Phase 6 — Testing & Defense
milestones.description: Weeks 19–20: APK release build, all chapters done, defense slides ready.

issues.title: [SETUP-01] Initialize GitHub monorepo structure
issues.body:
## User Story
As a developer, I want a clean monorepo so all three sub-projects (Flutter, Express, React) are version-controlled together.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root repo contains `/app`, `/backend`, `/web`, `/docs` folders
- [ ] `.gitignore` covers node_modules, .env, build/, .dart_tool/
- [ ] Branch protection enabled on `main` (require PR + 1 review)
- [ ] PR template created at `.github/pull_request_template.md`
- [ ] `README.md` at root with project overview and setup links

## Notes
Base your folder structure on the architecture doc. See `docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_System_Architecture.pdf`.

issues.title: [SETUP-02] Scaffold Flutter project and install dependencies
issues.body:
## User Story
As a Flutter developer, I want the project scaffolded with all dependencies so I can start building screens immediately.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `flutter create app` run, unnecessary boilerplate removed
- [ ] `pubspec.yaml` includes: google_ml_kit, flutter_tts, speech_to_text, image_picker, hive, hive_flutter, http, connectivity_plus, flutter_secure_storage
- [ ] Folder structure created: lib/core, lib/services, lib/screens, lib/widgets, lib/models
- [ ] `flutter run` launches on a physical Android device without errors
- [ ] `.env` file with TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_API_URL and `.env.example` committed

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [SETUP-03] Scaffold Express.js backend with modular structure
issues.body:
## User Story
As a backend developer, I want the Express project scaffolded with a feature-module structure so I can build each API module independently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm init` done, all dependencies installed: express, mongoose, jsonwebtoken, bcrypt, multer, cloudinary, openai, helmet, cors, express-rate-limit, joi, dotenv
- [ ] Folder structure: src/modules/, src/models/, src/middleware/, src/config/, src/utils/
- [ ] `src/app.js` sets up middleware chain: helmet → cors → rate-limit → json parser → routes
- [ ] `src/server.js` connects Mongoose then starts HTTP server
- [ ] `nodemon` dev script configured in package.json
- [ ] `.env.example` lists all required variables

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [SETUP-04] Connect MongoDB Atlas M0 cluster
issues.body:
## User Story
As a backend developer, I want MongoDB Atlas connected so all data persists to the cloud database.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Atlas M0 cluster created (free tier)
- [ ] IP allowlist configured (0.0.0.0/0 for dev, restrict for prod)
- [ ] Atlas user created with readWrite role
- [ ] `src/config/db.js` implements mongoose.connect() with poolSize:10 and reconnect handling
- [ ] Connection tested: `npm run dev` logs 'MongoDB connected'
- [ ] MONGO_URI added to .env.example

## Notes
M0 has 512MB limit and 100 max connections — sufficient for capstone demo.

issues.title: [SETUP-05] Scaffold React Vite app with TailwindCSS
issues.body:
## User Story
As a frontend developer, I want the React project scaffolded with Tailwind so I can build both the public site and admin panel.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `npm create vite@latest web -- --template react` run
- [ ] TailwindCSS 3 installed and configured (tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js)
- [ ] react-router-dom v6, axios, recharts, react-hook-form, date-fns installed
- [ ] Route structure defined: `/` (public), `/admin/*` (protected)
- [ ] `AuthContext.jsx` created with login/logout/currentUser
- [ ] `ProtectedRoute.jsx` blocks non-superadmin from /admin/*
- [ ] `VITE_API_URL` env var wired to axios base URL
- [ ] Deploys to Vercel with placeholder content

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [SETUP-06] Configure Cloudinary and GitHub Actions CI
issues.body:
## User Story
As the team PM, I want image storage and CI pipeline ready before feature development begins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cloudinary account created, API key/secret added to .env.example
- [ ] `src/config/cloudinary.js` exports configured v2 instance
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `npm test` on backend PRs
- [ ] Flutter CI: `flutter analyze` and `flutter test` on app/ PRs
- [ ] UptimeRobot free monitor configured to ping Render.com URL every 5 min (prevent cold start)

## Story Points: 2

issues.title: [SETUP-07] Write Chapter 1 draft (Introduction & Objectives)
issues.body:
## Task
Write the first thesis chapter based on the approved proposal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Background of the study (visual impairment in PH, existing app gaps)
- [ ] Statement of the problem (5–7 specific problems)
- [ ] Objectives (general + 5–6 specific, matching MOB/API/WEB features)
- [ ] Scope and limitations (ML Kit OCR limits, Filipino TTS device dependency, no iOS focus)
- [ ] Significance of the study (for NCDA, schools for the blind, PWD sector)
- [ ] Definition of terms (OCR, TTS, STT, MERN, Flutter, Accessibility)
- [ ] Saved to docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_Chapter1.docx

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [MOB-01] Implement Point-and-Read OCR (camera + ML Kit + TTS)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a visually impaired user, I want to point my phone camera at text and have it read aloud immediately, so I can understand printed content without sighted assistance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Camera opens on app launch via single tap or voice command 'scan'
- [ ] `image_picker` supports both camera capture and gallery selection
- [ ] `google_ml_kit` TextRecognitionV2 extracts text within 2 seconds for standard A4 at 12MP
- [ ] Extracted text is passed to TTS within 300ms of extraction completing
- [ ] If result is empty, TTS reads: 'Walang nahanap na teksto. Subukan ulit.'
- [ ] Works fully offline (no internet required for this feature)
- [ ] `OcrService` and `TtsService` are unit testable (mocks injectable)

## Tech
`google_ml_kit ^0.16` · `image_picker ^1.0` · `flutter_tts ^3.8`

## Story Points: 8

issues.title: [MOB-02] Implement Filipino/English TTS with language switching
issues.body:
## User Story
As a Filipino visually impaired user, I want the app to read text in Filipino so I can understand content in my native language without relying on poor English pronunciation of Tagalog words.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `flutter_tts` initialized with default language 'fil-PH'
- [ ] Language toggles between 'fil-PH' and 'en-US' via settings or voice command
- [ ] TTS speed adjustable from 0.5x to 2.0x, default 1.0x
- [ ] Voice pitch adjustable for user preference
- [ ] Runs fully offline using device Google TTS engine
- [ ] `TtsService.speak()`, `stop()`, `setLanguage()`, `setRate()` methods implemented
- [ ] Tested on Samsung and Xiaomi devices (common in PH market)

## Notes
iOS Siri TTS may not support Filipino. Document as limitation in Chapter 5.

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [MOB-03] Implement voice command navigation
issues.body:
## User Story
As a visually impaired user, I want to control the entire app using my voice so I never need to see or touch the screen.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `speech_to_text` listens continuously when app is in active mode
- [ ] Haptic buzz confirms when listening starts
- [ ] Commands recognized (Filipino and English variants):
  - 'scan' / 'kumuha' → open camera
  - 'basa' / 'read' → re-read current text
  - 'stop' → stop TTS
  - 'ipaliwanag' / 'explain' → trigger AI explain
  - 'i-save' / 'save' → save scan to history
  - 'history' → open history screen
  - 'ulit' / 'repeat' → repeat last TTS
  - 'settings' → open settings
- [ ] Unrecognized command → TTS: 'Hindi ko naintindihan. Sabihin ulit.'
- [ ] Command response latency under 1 second on mid-range Android
- [ ] RECORD_AUDIO permission requested on first launch with explanation

## Story Points: 8

issues.title: [MOB-04] Build gesture navigation UI with accessibility compliance
issues.body:
## User Story
As a visually impaired user, I want large, predictable gesture zones so I can navigate by touch without seeing the screen.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All interactive elements meet 48×48dp minimum (WCAG 2.5.5)
- [ ] Swipe left → previous scan in history
- [ ] Swipe right → next scan in history
- [ ] Double tap → repeat current TTS
- [ ] Long press on scan card → context menu (read, explain, save, delete) read aloud via TTS
- [ ] Haptic feedback on: app launch, scan capture, TTS start, command recognized, error
- [ ] All widgets have `Semantics` labels for TalkBack compatibility
- [ ] Tested eyes-closed: full scan → read → explain → save flow with zero screen viewing

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [MOB-05] Implement offline mode with local scan queue
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user in an area with no internet, I want OCR and TTS to work offline and my scans to sync when connectivity returns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MOB-01 OCR and MOB-02 TTS complete successfully with airplane mode enabled
- [ ] Offline scans stored in Hive box with `isSynced: false` flag
- [ ] `connectivity_plus` monitors network state
- [ ] When internet restored, `SyncService` uploads all pending scans to POST /api/scan/upload
- [ ] TTS announces: 'Na-sync na ang iyong mga scan.' when sync completes
- [ ] History screen shows unsynced scans with a pending indicator
- [ ] Sync handles partial failure gracefully (retries failed items only)

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [MOB-06] Build scan history screen
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want to browse and re-read my past scans so I can revisit documents without scanning them again.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Voice command 'history' navigates to history screen
- [ ] History items read aloud as numbered list on screen open
- [ ] User can say 'Item 3' or swipe to select a history item
- [ ] Selecting an item reads the extracted text aloud
- [ ] Paginated — fetches 20 items per page from GET /api/scan/history
- [ ] Delete via voice command 'burahin' or long-press gesture
- [ ] Offline-queued scans appear with pending badge

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [MOB-07] Build user settings screen
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user or caregiver, I want to adjust TTS speed, pitch, language, and font size so the app fits my specific accessibility needs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Settings accessible via voice command 'settings' or bottom nav
- [ ] TTS speed slider 0.5x–2.0x, default 1.0x — applied immediately on change
- [ ] Language toggle: Filipino / English
- [ ] Font size setting affects all on-screen text
- [ ] Settings cached in SharedPreferences locally
- [ ] On login, settings fetched from GET /api/user/profile and applied
- [ ] Changes synced to PATCH /api/user/settings

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [MOB-08] Flutter login screen and auth token management
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want to log in securely so my scan history and settings are saved to my personal account.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Login screen with email + password fields — large tap targets
- [ ] Calls POST /api/auth/login, stores JWT in `flutter_secure_storage`
- [ ] Invalid credentials → TTS: 'Mali ang email o password. Subukan ulit.'
- [ ] Token auto-attached to all `ApiService` requests via HTTP header
- [ ] On 401 response, user redirected to login screen
- [ ] Guest mode available (offline OCR + TTS without account)

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [MOB-09] Write OCR and TTS unit tests
issues.body:
## Task
Ensure core services are tested before backend integration begins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `test/ocr_service_test.dart` tests ML Kit extraction on 5 sample images (printed text, handwriting, signage, receipt, ID card)
- [ ] OCR accuracy ≥80% on printed text samples — document actual % in test output
- [ ] `test/tts_service_test.dart` verifies speak/stop cycle and language switching
- [ ] Tests run in CI via `flutter test` on PRs to `app/`
- [ ] Test results documented in `docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_Test_Results.pdf`

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [API-01] Build JWT authentication system (register + login)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want to register and log in securely so my data is private and tied to my account.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `User` Mongoose schema: name, email (unique), password (hashed), role enum (user|org_admin|superadmin), organizationId ref, settings subdoc
- [ ] POST /api/auth/register: validates body with Joi, hashes password bcrypt rounds=12, returns JWT
- [ ] POST /api/auth/login: finds user by email, bcrypt.compare, returns JWT (1h expiry)
- [ ] JWT_SECRET is 256-bit random string in .env — validated on startup
- [ ] Passwords never returned in any API response
- [ ] `verifyToken()` middleware attaches req.user to protected routes
- [ ] `requireRole(role)` middleware factory for RBAC

## Tech
`jsonwebtoken ^9` · `bcrypt ^5` · `joi ^17`

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [API-02] Build all Mongoose schemas (5 collections)
issues.body:
## Task
Create all MongoDB schemas before any feature modules are built.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `User.js` — name, email, password, role, organizationId, settings: {ttsSpeed, language, fontSize}, timestamps
- [ ] `ScanHistory.js` — userId ref, imageUrl, extractedText, ocrEngine enum (mlkit|vision), isSynced bool, timestamps. TTL index: 90 days
- [ ] `AiConversation.js` — userId ref, scanId ref, messages: [{role, content, timestamp}], model string, timestamps
- [ ] `Organization.js` — name, type enum, adminId ref, isActive bool, timestamps
- [ ] `Feedback.js` — userId ref, scanId ref, rating (1-5), issue enum, notes, status enum (open|reviewed|resolved), timestamps
- [ ] `AdminLog.js` — actor userId, action string, target string, timestamp. Append-only (no update/delete routes)
- [ ] Indexes added: userId on scan_history, organizationId on users, createdAt on admin_logs

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [API-03] Build scan management API (upload + history + delete)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a mobile app, I want to upload scans to the server and retrieve history so user data persists across devices and sessions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /api/scan/upload: accepts multipart image, uploads to Cloudinary (signed), saves ScanHistory doc, returns saved scan
- [ ] GET /api/scan/history: returns paginated (20/page) scans for req.user, newest first
- [ ] GET /api/scan/:id: returns single scan — validates ownership (userId match)
- [ ] DELETE /api/scan/:id: deletes MongoDB doc AND Cloudinary asset by public_id
- [ ] All routes require valid JWT
- [ ] `multer-storage-cloudinary` used for direct upload (no local disk)
- [ ] Response shape: `{ success: true, data: scan }` via utils/response.js

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [API-04] Build Google Vision OCR cloud fallback endpoint
issues.body:
## User Story
As the system, I want a cloud OCR fallback for complex documents where on-device ML Kit struggles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /api/scan/ocr-cloud: accepts multipart image, calls Google Vision TEXT_DETECTION
- [ ] Returns structured response: `{ text, confidence, engine: 'vision' }`
- [ ] GCP project created with billing account (required even for free tier)
- [ ] Hard $0 budget cap set in GCP Console to prevent charges
- [ ] Usage logged in ScanHistory.ocrEngine field
- [ ] If Vision API fails or quota exceeded → structured 503 with message
- [ ] Tested with: printed text, handwriting, low-light photo, receipt

## Notes
GCP billing account required for Vision API even on free tier (1,000 req/mo). Set hard budget cap.

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [API-05] Build AI proxy endpoint (OpenAI / Gemini)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want to ask questions about scanned text and receive plain-language explanations in Filipino or English.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /api/ai/explain: accepts { text, question, conversationId }
- [ ] System prompt instructs model to respond in same language as user's question
- [ ] Full AI response returned and saved to AiConversation collection
- [ ] GET /api/ai/history: returns user's conversation history
- [ ] API key stored server-side only — never exposed to Flutter client
- [ ] Rate limited to 20 requests/user/hour via express-rate-limit per userId
- [ ] If API unavailable → 503 with user-friendly message
- [ ] Tested with: Filipino questions, English questions, follow-up context

## Notes
Default to GPT-4o-mini. If budget is a concern, swap to Gemini 1.5 Flash (free 60 req/min).

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [API-06] Build user profile and settings API
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want my preferences saved to the server so they're restored on any device I log in from.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/user/profile: returns { name, email, role, organizationId, settings }
- [ ] PATCH /api/user/settings: accepts { ttsSpeed, language, fontSize }, validates with Joi, updates MongoDB
- [ ] PATCH /api/user/password: requires currentPassword confirmation before update
- [ ] Settings returned in login response for immediate app-side application
- [ ] All routes require valid JWT

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [API-07] Build superadmin management API
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want full visibility and control over all users and organizations in the system.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/admin/users: paginated, searchable, filterable by role/org
- [ ] PATCH /api/admin/user/:id: update role, isActive, organizationId
- [ ] GET /api/admin/organizations: all orgs with member count and admin info
- [ ] POST /api/admin/organizations: create new org tenant
- [ ] GET /api/admin/logs: audit log newest-first, filterable by action type
- [ ] GET /api/admin/stats: total users, scans today, AI queries today, active orgs
- [ ] All routes protected by requireRole('superadmin')
- [ ] Every admin action writes to AdminLog collection

## Story Points: 8

issues.title: [API-08] Build feedback API
issues.body:
## User Story
As a user, I want to report poor OCR results so the team can track and improve accuracy.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /api/feedback: accepts { scanId, rating (1-5), issue enum, notes } — requires JWT
- [ ] GET /api/feedback (admin/superadmin only): paginated, filterable by status
- [ ] PATCH /api/feedback/:id: admin updates status (open → reviewed → resolved)
- [ ] issue enum: wrong_text | poor_tts | app_crash | other
- [ ] Status transition validated (cannot go reviewed → open)

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [API-09] Configure security middleware stack (Helmet, CORS, rate limiting)
issues.body:
## Task
Harden the Express API before deployment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `helmet()` applied globally — sets CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.
- [ ] `cors()` allows only: Vercel React URL, localhost:5173 for dev. No wildcard origin in production.
- [ ] Global rate limit: 100 req/15min per IP via express-rate-limit
- [ ] Auth route rate limit: 10 req/15min per IP (prevents brute force)
- [ ] AI route rate limit: 20 req/hour per userId (cost control)
- [ ] All request bodies validated with Joi before controller logic
- [ ] 404 and global error handler middleware added

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [API-10] Deploy backend to Render.com and write Postman collection
issues.body:
## Task
Make the API publicly accessible and documented for the mobile and web teams.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Express app deployed to Render.com free tier
- [ ] All environment variables set in Render dashboard
- [ ] UptimeRobot monitor pings /api/health every 5 minutes
- [ ] GET /api/health endpoint returns 200 with { status: 'ok', timestamp }
- [ ] Postman collection created: all 20+ endpoints with sample bodies, auth headers, env variables
- [ ] Collection exported to `docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_API_Collection.json`
- [ ] Postman environment file includes: BASE_URL, JWT_TOKEN variables

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [WEB-01] Build public landing page (GCash-style info site)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a stakeholder or panel member, I want a polished public website so I can understand what TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst is and download the APK.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hero: app name, tagline 'Marinig ang Mundo', Download APK CTA button
- [ ] Features grid: 6 cards — Point & Read, AI Explain, Filipino TTS, Voice Control, Offline Mode, Scan History
- [ ] How It Works: 3-step illustrated flow (Capture → Extract → Listen)
- [ ] About section: project background, NU branding, team member names, client organization logo
- [ ] FAQ accordion: 6 questions about accessibility features and system requirements
- [ ] Footer: team names, course, academic year, NU logo, GitHub repo link
- [ ] Lighthouse performance ≥85, accessibility ≥90
- [ ] Deployed to Vercel with custom domain (optional)
- [ ] Fully responsive — mobile, tablet, desktop

## Story Points: 8

issues.title: [WEB-02] Build superadmin authentication and protected routing
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want a secure login portal so only authorized administrators can access the management dashboard.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Login page at /admin/login — email + password, large submit button
- [ ] Calls POST /api/auth/login, stores JWT in React state (NOT localStorage)
- [ ] ProtectedRoute blocks /admin/* if no token or role !== 'superadmin'
- [ ] Axios interceptor attaches Authorization: Bearer {token} to all requests
- [ ] On 401 response, redirect to /admin/login with toast: 'Session expired.'
- [ ] Logout clears token from state and redirects to /admin/login
- [ ] AdminLayout component wraps all /admin/* pages with sidebar + topbar

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [WEB-03] Build admin dashboard with metrics and charts
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want a dashboard overview so I can monitor platform health at a glance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metric cards: Total Users, Scans Today, AI Queries Today, Active Organizations
- [ ] Line chart: scans per day for last 30 days (recharts LineChart)
- [ ] Bar chart: top 5 most active users by scan count
- [ ] Recent feedback table: last 5 open items with status badges
- [ ] All data from GET /api/admin/stats — loading skeleton shown while fetching
- [ ] Error state shown if API is unavailable
- [ ] Data auto-refreshes every 60 seconds

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [WEB-04] Build user management table (CRUD + role assignment)
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want to manage all users — view, search, assign roles, and deactivate — from one screen.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Searchable data table: Name, Email, Role, Organization, Status, Joined date
- [ ] Role dropdown per row: user | org_admin | superadmin — triggers PATCH /api/admin/user/:id
- [ ] Active/Inactive toggle — inactive users cannot log in
- [ ] Pagination: 25 per page with prev/next controls and total count
- [ ] Client-side CSV export of current filtered results
- [ ] Confirmation modal before role change or deactivation

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [WEB-05] Build organization/tenancy management
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want to create and manage organizations so institutional clients can use TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst with their own admin.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Org list table: Name, Type, Admin, Member Count, Created Date
- [ ] Create org modal: name, type (school|ngo|government|other), assign org_admin from existing users
- [ ] Click org row → drill-down: member list, roles, scan activity
- [ ] Deactivate org button — all members blocked until reactivated
- [ ] Calls GET/POST /api/admin/organizations

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [WEB-06] Build scan logs viewer and feedback management
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want to review all scan records and user feedback so I can monitor OCR quality and resolve issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
**Scan Logs:**
- [ ] Table: User, Org, OCR Engine, Text Preview (50 chars), Date
- [ ] Filters: date range, user search, OCR engine (mlkit|vision)
- [ ] Click row → modal with full Cloudinary image + full extracted text

**Feedback Management:**
- [ ] Table: User, Issue Type, Rating (stars), Status badge, Date
- [ ] Filter by status: All | Open | Reviewed | Resolved
- [ ] Click row → detail panel with notes + linked scan preview
- [ ] PATCH /api/feedback/:id to update status — confirmation toast

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [WEB-07] Build audit log viewer
issues.body:
## User Story
As a superadmin, I want to see a complete audit trail of system actions for accountability and institutional reporting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read-only table: Actor, Action, Target, Timestamp
- [ ] Filter by action type: login | role_change | delete | org_change
- [ ] Newest entries first, paginated 50/page
- [ ] CSV export for institutional audit reporting
- [ ] No edit or delete controls on the log view

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [WEB-08] Deploy React web portal to Vercel
issues.body:
## Task
Make both the public site and admin portal publicly accessible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub repo connected to Vercel (auto-deploy on push to main)
- [ ] VITE_API_URL env var set to Render.com backend URL
- [ ] Public site accessible at Vercel URL
- [ ] /admin/login accessible and functional against live API
- [ ] Lighthouse run on public site — performance ≥85, accessibility ≥90
- [ ] Optional: custom domain configured (.com.ph)

## Story Points: 2

issues.title: [INT-01] Build Flutter AI explain chat screen
issues.body:
## User Story
As a visually impaired user, I want to ask questions about my scanned text and hear AI explanations in my language so I can understand complex content.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Triggered by voice command 'ipaliwanag' / 'explain' after any scan
- [ ] Calls POST /api/ai/explain with current scan text + user question
- [ ] AI response displayed on screen AND read aloud via TTS automatically
- [ ] Conversation context maintained for follow-up questions in same session
- [ ] Loading indicator + TTS 'Naglo-load...' while waiting for response
- [ ] Error state if offline or API fails — TTS reads fallback message
- [ ] Conversation history viewable via GET /api/ai/history

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [INT-02] Full end-to-end integration test (Flutter → Express → MongoDB → React)
issues.body:
## Task
Verify the complete system works together before final testing phase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E flow tested: camera scan → ML Kit OCR → upload to Express → save to MongoDB → appear in React admin scan logs
- [ ] AI flow tested: scan → voice command → Express AI proxy → GPT response → TTS readout
- [ ] Auth flow: register via API → login in Flutter → access history → visible in React admin user table
- [ ] Offline flow: scan without internet → sync when connected → appears in history
- [ ] All API endpoints return correct responses with Flutter as the HTTP client (not Postman)
- [ ] Issues logged and triaged — critical bugs fixed before Phase 6

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [INT-03] Performance and accessibility audit
issues.body:
## Task
Measure and document system performance to include in Chapter 4 results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OCR latency: measure on 10 sample images — target <2s, document actual avg
- [ ] API response time: measure /api/scan/upload and /api/ai/explain — target <500ms and <1s respectively
- [ ] TTS startup: measure time from text-ready to first spoken word — target <300ms
- [ ] Flutter app tested eyes-closed: full feature flow with zero screen viewing
- [ ] TalkBack compatibility verified on Android — all Semantics labels announced
- [ ] React Lighthouse: public site ≥85 performance, ≥90 accessibility
- [ ] All results tabulated and saved to docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_Test_Results.pdf

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [DEF-01] APK release build and sideload testing
issues.body:
## Task
Produce the final signed APK deliverable for panel demonstration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `flutter build apk --release` completes without errors
- [ ] APK signed with a keystore (document keystore location safely)
- [ ] APK sideloaded and tested on at least 2 physical Android devices
- [ ] Full demo flow works on release build: scan → TTS → AI explain → history → admin portal
- [ ] APK file size documented
- [ ] APK download link added to public React website

## Story Points: 3

issues.title: [DEF-02] Finalize thesis chapters 2–5
issues.body:
## Task
Complete all remaining thesis documentation for NU submission.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Chapter 2: Review of Related Literature — 15–20 sources, local and foreign, with synthesis paragraph
- [ ] Chapter 3: Methodology — Agile SDLC narrative, system design diagrams, tools justification, testing approach
- [ ] Chapter 4: Results and Discussion — screenshots, OCR accuracy table, AI response samples, Lighthouse scores, user testing summary
- [ ] Chapter 5: Conclusions and Recommendations — conclusions per objective, limitations, future work (live OCR, sign language, iOS)
- [ ] All documents saved to docs/ folder
- [ ] Reviewed by all team members before submission

## Story Points: 8

issues.title: [DEF-03] Prepare defense slides and demo script
issues.body:
## Task
Prepare all panel defense materials.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Defense slides (docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_DefenseSlides.pptx) cover: problem, novelty, solution, architecture, demo flow, results, Q&A
- [ ] Demo script written: timed 5-minute flow — camera scan → TTS → 'Ipaliwanag' → AI response → admin portal review
- [ ] Sprint burndown screenshots captured from Agile board and included in Chapter 3
- [ ] Q&A prep document: anticipated panel questions on novelty, security, scalability, ML Kit limits
- [ ] 2 mock panel rehearsals conducted with classmates acting as panel
- [ ] All team members can explain any part of the system

## Story Points: 5

issues.title: [DEF-04] Create project ERD and system wireframes
issues.body:
## Task
Produce the visual design artifacts required for Chapter 3 and panel review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ERD diagram: all 6 MongoDB collections with field types, data types, and relationship lines — saved as docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_ERD.png
- [ ] Flutter wireframes: scan screen, history screen, AI chat screen, settings screen, login screen — annotated with accessibility notes
- [ ] Wireframes saved to docs/TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst_Wireframes.pdf
- [ ] Both documents included in Chapter 3 methodology section

## Story Points: 3

project.name: TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst Kanban Board
project.body: Agile project board for TxtRecIntSrtHlpAst (Text Recognition and Intelligent Speech Helper Assistant) — National University Philippines BSIT Capstone. Tracks all 6 phases: Setup → Mobile → Backend → React Web → AI Integration → Defense.
